info.name: FixMate API
info.description:
# FixMate API

**FixMate is a home-services marketplace.** A customer browses service categories, picks a technician's service, and books a time. The technician accepts the request; the customer pays for it through **Stripe Checkout**; the technician then carries the job through to completion, and the customer leaves a review. Admins oversee the whole system — they manage users, categories, and every booking.

Payments are handled with **Stripe** (test mode) end to end — a real Checkout session is created and a signed Stripe webhook confirms it.

---

## How this collection is organised (by module, not by role)

Every folder is a **resource/module**, and it contains **all** operations on that resource — create, read, update, delete, and the role-restricted ones — together in one place. You never leave the `Categories` folder to create a category, or hop to an `Admin` folder to see all bookings. Which role a request needs is written on each request; the grouping stays by module.

Each folder's **description** (this info panel, one level down) lists its endpoints in a small table: method, path, who can call it, and what it does. Open a folder to see its module doc.

---

## Roles — who can do what

| Capability | Customer | Technician | Admin |
|---|:---:|:---:|:---:|
| Register / login / manage own profile & password | ✅ | ✅ | login only |
| Browse categories / services / technicians | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Create a booking | ✅ | — | — |
| Cancel own booking (before IN_PROGRESS) | ✅ | — | — |
| Pay for a booking (Stripe) | ✅ | — | — |
| Review a completed booking | ✅ | — | — |
| See own bookings | ✅ (as customer) | ✅ (assigned) | — |
| Accept / decline / progress / complete a booking | — | ✅ | — |
| Manage own technician profile & availability | — | ✅ | — |
| See ALL users / bookings | — | — | ✅ |
| Ban / unban a user | — | — | ✅ |
| Create categories | — | — | ✅ |

---

## Seeded accounts (run `npm run seed` once against the target database)

| Role | Email | Password |
|---|---|---|
| Admin | `admin@fixmate.dev` (or your `ADMIN_EMAIL`) | your `ADMIN_PASSWORD` |
| Technician (sample) | `sam.technician@fixmate.dev` | `Secret123!` |

The seed also creates a `Plumbing` category and a `Kitchen sink repair` service under the sample technician, so `Bookings > Create` has something real to book immediately.

---

## Variables & auth

All variables (`baseUrl`, `accessToken`, `bookingId`, …) are **collection-scoped** — they live only inside this collection and won't collide with any other project's Postman globals. Just import and run; no separate environment file.

`Login` and `Register` auto-save the tokens and the relevant id into collection variables via a Test script, and every protected request sends `Authorization: Bearer {{accessToken}}` — you never copy a token by hand. Run `Login` for whichever role you want to act as; it overwrites `accessToken` for subsequent requests. `Bookings > Create`, `Payments > Create`, and `Categories > Create` similarly save `bookingId` / `paymentId` / `categoryId`.

---

## A full run-through

1. **User > Register** (or use a seeded account), then **Auth > Login** as that role.
2. **Categories / Services > Get All (public)** and **User > Get All Technicians (public)** — browse.
3. **Bookings > Create** (as customer) → login as the technician → **Bookings > Update Status (technician)** = `ACCEPTED`.
4. Login as the customer → **Payments > Create** → pay the returned `checkoutUrl` with Stripe test card `4242 4242 4242 4242`.
5. Login as technician → push status `IN_PROGRESS` → `COMPLETED`.
6. Login as customer → **Reviews > Create**.
7. Login as admin → **User / Bookings > Get All (admin)** to oversee everything.

---

## Error shape

Every error returns the same JSON:
```json
{ "success": false, "message": "...", "errorDetails": null }
```
Validation failures populate `errorDetails` with a list of `{ field, message }`.

item.name: Auth
item.description:
# Auth

Session lifecycle for an existing account: log in, refresh the token, log out, read the current user. Creating an account is in **User** (it creates the user record).

| Method | Path | Who can call | What it does |
|---|---|---|---|
| `POST` | `/api/auth/login` | public | Exchange email + password for access + refresh tokens (auto-saved). |
| `POST` | `/api/auth/refresh-token` | valid refresh cookie | Issue a new access token. |
| `POST` | `/api/auth/logout` | public | Clear the auth cookies. |
| `GET` | `/api/auth/me` | any logged-in user | Return the authenticated account. |

request.description:
**Who can call this:** anyone (public).

Active body: CUSTOMER. To log in as a different role, delete the active JSON block and uncomment the TECHNICIAN or ADMIN block below it (they're valid JSON once uncommented — the `//` lines above/below them are the only thing making them inactive).

Saves `accessToken` + `refreshToken` + the matching id into collection variables automatically, based on the `role` in the response.

**Who can call this:** anyone with a valid `refreshToken` cookie (set automatically by Postman's cookie jar after Login runs in the same session).

Issues a new `accessToken` only — the refresh token itself is not rotated.
**Who can call this:** anyone (public). Clears the `accessToken`/`refreshToken` cookies server-side.
**Who can call this:** any authenticated user, any role. Identical to `User > Get My Profile (me)` — kept here too since the assignment spec documents it at this exact path.

item.name: User
item.description:
# User

Every operation on a **user record** — whoever performs it. Self-service (any role), a technician editing their own technician-profile & availability, admin user-management, and the public browsing of technician users. Role is noted per request in `(parentheses)`; it is never a separate folder.

| Method | Path | Who can call | What it does |
|---|---|---|---|
| `POST` | `/api/users/register` | public | Create a CUSTOMER (default) or TECHNICIAN account. |
| `GET` | `/api/users/me` | any logged-in user | Return own profile. |
| `PATCH` | `/api/users/update-profile` | any logged-in user | Update own name/phone + bio/photo/address. |
| `PATCH` | `/api/users/update-password` | any logged-in user | Change own password. |
| `PUT` | `/api/technician/profile` | technician | Update own technician profile (skills, experience, availability flag). |
| `PUT` | `/api/technician/availability` | technician | Replace own weekly availability schedule. |
| `GET` | `/api/technicians` | public | Browse all active technician users. |
| `GET` | `/api/technicians/:id` | public | Get one technician user with services + reviews. |
| `GET` | `/api/admin/users` | admin | List every user of any role. |
| `PATCH` | `/api/admin/users/:id` | admin | Ban / unban a user (immediate). |

request.description:
**Who can call this:** anyone (public).

Active body: CUSTOMER (role omitted -> defaults to CUSTOMER). Uncomment the TECHNICIAN block to register a technician instead — it also auto-creates an empty Technician Profile.

Does **not** auto-login — run `Auth > Login` afterward to get tokens.
**Who can call this:** any authenticated user, any role. Returns whichever account's `accessToken` is currently saved in the collection variables.
**Who can call this:** any authenticated user, any role. All fields optional — `name`/`phone` update the User row, `bio`/`profilePhoto`/`address` update the Profile row.

**Who can call this:** any authenticated user, any role. After running this, `Auth > Login`'s saved password will be stale for this account — log in with `NewSecret123!` afterward, or skip this request during a full run-through.
**Who can call this:** TECHNICIAN only. All fields optional — send only what changes.

**Who can call this:** TECHNICIAN only.

Returns the logged-in technician's own availability slots, sorted by date then start time. Each time comes back rich: `{ display: "09:00 AM", hour: 9, minute: 0, period: "AM", time24: "09:00" }`.

**Who can call this:** TECHNICIAN only.

Replaces the technician's ENTIRE availability with the slots you send (not additive). Each slot is a **single calendar day**:
- `date`: `"YYYY-MM-DD"`.
- `startTime` / `endTime`: **12-hour** `"hh:mm AM/PM"` (e.g. `"09:00 AM"`, `"05:30 PM"`). Malformed values like `"017:00"` or `"13:00 PM"` are rejected. `endTime` must be after `startTime` on that day.

**Rules enforced:**
- Past dates are rejected (`400 "Slot date is in the past"`, listing the offending slot in readable form).
- No two slots may overlap on the same date. On conflict → `400 "Slot overlaps with an existing slot"` and `errorDetails` names BOTH colliding slots, e.g. `{ "slot": "Aug 15, 2026 09:00 AM–05:00 PM", "conflictsWith": "Aug 15, 2026 04:00 PM–06:00 PM" }`.
- Touching slots (one ends 12:00 PM, next starts 12:00 PM) do NOT conflict.
- At least one slot is required (an empty array is rejected).

The response echoes each slot in a rich form: `startTime: { display, hour, minute, period, time24 }` so a frontend can populate dropdowns without re-parsing.
**Who can call this:** anyone (public). Only returns users with `role: TECHNICIAN` and `activeStatus: ACTIVE`.
**Who can call this:** anyone (public). Includes the technician's active services and every review they've received.
**Who can call this:** ADMIN only. Returns every user, any role, password always omitted.

**Who can call this:** ADMIN only. Only toggles `activeStatus` — this is not a general user editor (name/email/role are not editable here, by design).

Defaults to targeting `{{customerId}}` — change the URL's id to ban/unban a technician instead by pasting a different user id.

Verified to take effect **immediately**: a banned user's already-issued access token stops working on their very next request, and they cannot log in again while blocked.

item.name: Categories
item.description:
# Categories

Every operation on the **category** resource. Public read; admin create/list. All here regardless of role.

| Method | Path | Who can call | What it does |
|---|---|---|---|
| `GET` | `/api/categories` | public | List all categories (the one customers/technicians browse). |
| `POST` | `/api/admin/categories` | admin | Create a category; 409 on duplicate name. Saves categoryId. |
| `GET` | `/api/admin/categories` | admin | Same list under the admin prefix. |

request.description: **Who can call this:** anyone (public).
**Who can call this:** ADMIN only. Fails with 409 if a category with this exact `name` already exists. Saves the new category's id as `categoryId`.
**Who can call this:** ADMIN only. Same data as the public `Categories > Get All` — exists so an admin dashboard doesn't need to hit the public route.

item.name: Services
item.description:
# Services

Every operation on the **service** resource. Public read; a technician creates/updates/deletes their own service listings. Role noted per request — not a separate folder.

| Method | Path | Who can call | What it does |
|---|---|---|---|
| `GET` | `/api/services` | public | List active services (filter categoryId, searchTerm). |
| `GET` | `/api/services/:id` | public | Get one service. |
| `POST` | `/api/technician/services` | technician | Create a service listing; 404 if category missing. Saves serviceId. |
| `PATCH` | `/api/technician/services/:id` | technician (owner) | Update own service; 403 if not the owner. |
| `DELETE` | `/api/technician/services/:id` | technician (owner) | Delete own service (deactivates if it has bookings). |

request.description: **Who can call this:** anyone (public). Filter by `categoryId`, search by `searchTerm` (matches title/description).
**Who can call this:** anyone (public).

**Who can call this:** TECHNICIAN only.

Creates a service the technician offers. `categoryId` must reference an existing category (404 otherwise). The service is owned by the logged-in technician. Saves the new id as `serviceId`.
**Who can call this:** TECHNICIAN only, and only the owner of the service (403 otherwise). All fields optional — send only what changes. Changing `categoryId` re-validates the new category exists.

**Who can call this:** TECHNICIAN only, and only the owner (403 otherwise). If the service has no bookings it is hard-deleted. If it has booking history it is **deactivated** (`isActive: false`) instead — so it disappears from public browsing while its bookings keep their record.

item.name: Bookings
item.description:
# Bookings

Every operation on the **booking** resource, for every role that touches it. `List Mine (customer)` = the caller's own bookings as a customer; `List Assigned (technician)` = bookings assigned to the caller as technician; `List All (admin)` = every booking platform-wide.

| Method | Path | Who can call | What it does |
|---|---|---|---|
| `POST` | `/api/bookings` | customer | Create a booking; technician derived from the service. Saves bookingId. |
| `GET` | `/api/bookings` | customer | List the caller's own bookings. |
| `GET` | `/api/bookings/:id` | customer (owner) | Get one of the caller's own bookings. |
| `PATCH` | `/api/bookings/:id/cancel` | customer (owner) | Cancel before IN_PROGRESS. |
| `GET` | `/api/technician/bookings` | technician | List bookings assigned to the caller. |
| `PATCH` | `/api/technician/bookings/:id` | technician (assigned) | Drive the state machine: accept/decline/start/complete. |
| `GET` | `/api/admin/bookings` | admin | List every booking platform-wide. |

request.description:
**Who can call this:** CUSTOMER only.

`serviceId` must reference an existing, active Service. The technician is derived automatically from the service — you never send `technicianId`. Saves the new booking's id as `bookingId`.
**Who can call this:** CUSTOMER only. Returns only bookings belonging to the logged-in customer.
**Who can call this:** CUSTOMER only, and only the customer who owns this booking (403 otherwise).

**Who can call this:** CUSTOMER only, and only the owner. Only allowed while status is `REQUESTED`, `ACCEPTED`, or `PAID` — fails with 400 once `IN_PROGRESS` or later, matching the spec's cancellation rule.
**Who can call this:** TECHNICIAN only. Returns only bookings assigned to the logged-in technician.

**Who can call this:** TECHNICIAN only, and only the technician assigned to this booking (403 otherwise).

See the body comment for the full legal-transition table. This single endpoint replaces what would otherwise be four separate accept/decline/start/complete routes.
**Who can call this:** ADMIN only. Returns every booking in the system regardless of which customer/technician it belongs to.

item.name: Payments
item.description:
# Payments

Every operation on the **payment** resource. Paying for an accepted booking via Stripe, plus reading payment records.

| Method | Path | Who can call | What it does |
|---|---|---|---|
| `POST` | `/api/payments/create` | customer (owner) | Open a Stripe checkout for an ACCEPTED booking. Returns checkoutUrl, saves paymentId. |
| `POST` | `/api/payments/confirm` | stripe (webhook) | Signed webhook Stripe calls after payment; moves booking to PAID. |
| `GET` | `/api/payments` | customer | List the caller's own payments. |
| `GET` | `/api/payments/:id` | customer (owner) | Get one payment the caller owns. |

request.description:
**Who can call this:** CUSTOMER only, and only the owner of the booking. Booking must be status `ACCEPTED` (a Technician must accept it first via `Technician Self-Service > Update Booking Status`).

Returns a real Stripe test-mode `checkoutUrl` — open it in a browser and pay with card `4242 4242 4242 4242` to complete it.

**Who can call this:** Stripe only. This is a webhook endpoint, not a normal request — it verifies a cryptographic signature over the raw body and will reject any hand-typed body. Included for reference only. To actually trigger it: run `npm run stripe:webhook` (starts the Stripe CLI listener) and complete a real payment via the `checkoutUrl` from `Create`.
**Who can call this:** CUSTOMER only. Returns only payments on bookings the logged-in customer owns.
**Who can call this:** CUSTOMER only, and only the owner of the underlying booking.

item.name: Reviews
item.description:
# Reviews

Every operation on the **review** resource. A customer reviews a completed booking; one review per booking.

| Method | Path | Who can call | What it does |
|---|---|---|---|
| `POST` | `/api/reviews` | customer (owner) | Leave a 1–5 rating + comment on a COMPLETED booking. 409 on a second review. |

request.description: **Who can call this:** CUSTOMER only, and only the owner of the booking. Booking must be status `COMPLETED`. `rating` must be an integer 1-5. One review per booking (enforced by a unique constraint) — a second attempt returns 409.
